figures.caption: Figure 1: On the left, a control volume ωc, with its barycenter and edge midpoints. In violet, the sub-cell ωpc which results by connecting the vertex with position xp, the cell barycenter of coordinates xbc = 1/(d+1) ∑ p∈P(c) xp and the left and right midpoints of the edges impinging on node p, namely p−1/2 and p+1/2. In the middle, the dual cell ωp (cyan) is obtained by the union of all sub-cells ωc sharing a generic mesh node p. On the right, the notation for the boundary dual cell located on ∂Ω.

Figure 3: Numerical solution of the Taylor-Green vortex at the final time tf = 0.25 obtained using our structure-preserving semi-implicit scheme for the incompressible Navier-Stokes equations with implicit viscosity. Total momentum (left), time evolution of the kinetic energy integrated over Ω (center) and time evolution of the L2 norm of the divergence of the velocity field (right).

Figure 6: Numerical solution of the MHD vortex ad a final time tf = 0.25 using the structure preserving semi-implicit scheme. Pressure contours (top left) and magnetic field contours (top right). Time evolution of the kinetic and magnetic energy integrated over Ω (bottom left). Time evolution of the L2 error norm of the divergence of the velocity and of the divergence of the magnetic field (bottom right).

Figure 2: Numerical solution of the Taylor-Green vortex at the final time tf = 0.25 obtained using our structure-preserving semi-implicit scheme. Pressure contours (top left), 1D cut along the line y = π for the velocity component u and along the line x = π for v and comparison with the exact solution (top right). Time evolution of the kinetic energy integrated over Ω (bottom left) and time evolution of the L2 norm of the divergence of the velocity field (bottom right).

Figure 7: Numerical solution of the Solid rotor problem at the final time tf = 0.25 obtained using our structure-preserving semi-implicit scheme. Pressure contours (top left), time evolution of the kinetic energy integrated over Ω (top right). Time evolution of the L2 norm of the divergence of the velocity field and (bottom left) and curl of the distortion field (bottom right).

Figure 5: Numerical solution of the Gresho vortex at the final time tf = 0.25 obtained using our structurepreserving semi-implicit scheme for the compressible Euler equations. Pressure contours (left), velocity magnitude (center) and time evolution of the kinetic energy integrated over Ω (right).

Figure 4: Numerical solution of the Sod shock tube problem obtained using our structure-preserving semiimplicit scheme for compressible Euler equations. 1D cut along the line y = 0 for the density (left) and for the momentum (right) at tf = 0.25.
